source_baseline.reason: Procedural blockout validates GLB loading only. It has visible intersection risk, weak silhouette design, and fake state logic.

iteration_rule: Never treat one generated model as final. Each candidate must pass silhouette, spatial logic, material separation, and state-anchor checks before web implementation.

candidates.label: Glass Root Specimen
candidates.intent: Primary model candidate: the garden becomes a transparent technical specimen object with roots protected inside glass. It is quieter, more premium, and less likely to read as fantasy terrain.
candidates.model_prompt: Single centered GLB-ready 3D asset, premium transparent glass root specimen, clear oval glass vessel containing a compact dark mineral garden core, delicate root network suspended inside the glass, subtle cyan data veins running through the roots, three tiny warm amber signal buds inside the vessel, scientific botanical archive object, quiet luxury museum lighting response, clean premium silhouette, no background scene, no text, no logo, no characters, no mascot.
candidates.negative_prompt: No bottle label, no medicine vial, no perfume bottle, no text, no logo, no brand, no character, no fantasy terrarium, no soil clump mess, no house, no landscape, no oversized plant, no decorative flowers, no unclear glass blob, no roots escaping randomly through solid glass, no roots piercing vessel walls.

spatial_contract.primary_mass: one transparent glass vessel with a compact dark mineral garden core inside
spatial_contract.root_logic: roots are fully contained and visible through glass; they curve around the core and must not pierce vessel walls
spatial_contract.route_logic: cyan signal lines run along internal roots or inside designed glass channels
spatial_contract.blossom_logic: amber buds stay internal, tiny, and signal-like; they attach to root endpoints or core sockets

candidates.label: Mineral Root Island
candidates.intent: Secondary fallback: a floating mineral island with roots and route veins, but designed as a specimen object rather than fantasy terrain.
candidates.model_prompt: Single centered GLB-ready 3D asset, floating mineral evidence garden island, asymmetric oval graphite and basalt base, carved shallow root sockets under the island, translucent glass roots emerging from visible sockets without intersecting the stone, thin cyan luminous route veins embedded into the top surface, three small amber data blossoms attached to vein endpoints, technical botanical museum specimen, clean silhouette, no background environment, no text, no logo, no characters, no mascot.
candidates.negative_prompt: No fantasy landscape, no cute garden, no oversized flowers, no grass carpet, no tree, no house, no path, no character, no mascot, no text, no logo, no UI, no dashboard, no brand mark, no random floating debris, no merged roots passing through solid stone.

spatial_contract.primary_mass: one asymmetric mineral island with a flat-enough top surface and visible underside
spatial_contract.root_logic: roots must originate from underside sockets; roots may overlap visually but should not pass through the island core
spatial_contract.route_logic: cyan veins should be inlaid on the surface, not floating above it
spatial_contract.blossom_logic: blossoms attach to route endpoints and stay small; they should read as data nodes, not flowers
state_anchors: upper empty space for archive particles

candidates.label: Technical Data Island
candidates.intent: Less organic and more technical: the garden is a compact island-machine with root-like conduits and plate layers.
candidates.model_prompt: Single centered GLB-ready 3D asset, compact technical data island, dark ceramic mineral core with layered graphite plates, root-like translucent conduits emerging from underside ports, thin cyan circuit-like route veins embedded on top, three small amber data capsules rising from the surface, refined industrial botanical hybrid, precise clean silhouette, no background scene, no text, no logo, no characters, no mascot.
candidates.negative_prompt: No spaceship, no generic sci-fi drone, no dashboard UI, no labels, no text, no logo, no brand mark, no character, no mascot, no messy cables piercing plates, no random mechanical parts, no weapon-like silhouette.

spatial_contract.primary_mass: one compact layered island-machine with clear top, side, and underside
spatial_contract.root_logic: conduits originate from ports and remain below the object plane
spatial_contract.route_logic: top veins should align with plate grooves and read as embedded channels
spatial_contract.blossom_logic: amber capsules rise from designed sockets; no floating unsupported pods

review_loop: Run it through model candidate lab.
Reject if silhouette fails before material cleanup.
Reject if roots, routes, or blossoms do not attach to believable sockets.
Only after spatial logic passes, add scan, bloom, and archive animation layers.
